channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Slay the Spire 2 Roadmap Prioritizes Healthy Workflow Over Dates
item.content:encoded:
Slay the Spire 2 is trying to stay ahead of unkept promises by issuing a roadmap without firm delivery dates.

Slay the Spire 2 has had strong success since its debut in Steam Early Access in March 2025. It still sees a high player count with nearly 300,000 concurrent players in the last 24 hours at the time of this writing. Within one month, the game even sold 5 million copies.

Mega Crit’s Slay the Spire 2 Roadmap

Released as part of the April 2026 Newosletter from Mega Crit, the roadmap promises new content, including two alternate acts, a new character, and more cards. Some of the goals for the far future include “true victory.” These are mentioned alongside ongoing updates to quality-of-life features and bug fixes.

The full roadmap can be seen below:

Features and Systems:
– Steam Workshop support
– Support for more languages
– The Bestiary, a way to view and learn about the enemies in the game
– Experimental game modes

Ongoing Tasks:
– Fixings bugs
– Compatibility and performance improvements
– Game balance and tweaks
– Quality of life features
– Audio polish (more silly voices)
– Visual polish (less silly placeholder art)

Content (No spoilers, it’s for your own good!)
– Alternate Act 2
– New Character
– Alternate Act 3
– More cards, events, relics, and potions

Further off in the Future
– Ports to other consoles/platforms/mobile/etc
– Steam Achievements & Tradings Cards
– “True Victory” + Everything that comes with it

No Firm Release Dates for These Slay the Spire 2 Changes

Casey from Mega Crit also addressed the lack of release dates. In their statement, they discuss being a small development team and choosing to work at a healthy pace rather than overly promising a firm timeline.

“We evaluate our tasks each week and work on what feels most impactful. It’s not the most organized method, but this allows spontaneous experimentation like the dialogues you have with the Ancients or the existence of a Room Full of Cheese. We won’t massively expand the size of the studio to finish the game faster. Exacting deadlines produce sloppy uninspired work and I don’t want Sloppy Spire 2, I want Slay the Spire 2,” the statement reads.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor this game’s development and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Have you checked out Mega Crit’s Slay the Spire 2 yet? If so, what do you think? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/slay-the-spire-2-roadmap-prioritizes-healthy-workflow-over-dates/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 Coming in 2027
item.content:encoded:
Bandai Namco has officially announced Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3. First revealed as Age 1000 in January. Many players initially expected the game to be a follow-up to Dragon Ball Online, a 2010 MMORPG, but it has now been confirmed to be the next Xenoverse title.

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 was initially leaked in February after a social media blunder on Bandai Namco’s Southeast Asian YouTube page. Now, however, Bandai has revealed the full trailer and announced that the game is coming in 2027.

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 Trailer Reveal

The first trailer for the next Xenoverse game was nearly 4 minutes long and featured English voice acting for Bulma, as well as several stand-ins for custom character creations. Of course, the Xenoverse line is all about players creating their own characters and inserting them into the Dragon Ball Z universe.

The Game Features Original Work From Akira Toriyama

Despite his unfortunate passing in 2024, Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 will feature work from the creator of the Dragon Ball series, Akira Toriyama.

It had previously been stated that work on the project began in the late 2010s and that more than half a decade had been invested in the game, including never-before-seen original characters from Toriyama and key world-building elements. His name is even featured early in the trailer.

Although Bulma is the only mainstay Dragon Ball character featured in the trailer, Piccolo is also teased for a second. In one shot, around the 30-second mark, you can see what looks to be Piccolo’s cape from the back.

It will have been a full decade since the release of Dragon Ball Xenoverse 2 by the time this new game releases. The last title was released in October 2016 and has received several quality-of-life updates to ensure its longevity.

Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3 is set for release on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam in 2027. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

For more Dragon Ball on Insider Gaming, check out the upcoming DLC for Dragon Ball Sparking Zero.

What are you hoping to see from Dragon Ball Xenoverse 3? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/dragon-ball-xenoverse-3-coming-in-2027/

item.title: Modder Discovers Abandoned Dark Souls II Level: Even More Sewers
item.content:encoded:
The Dark Souls series has hosted no shortage of damp, dank, poisonous places. According to modder and YouTuber Doneda, we only saw the tip of the iceberg. In a new video, we’re given a tour of what was originally imagined for Dark Souls 2’s “The Gutter.”

Dark Souls 2’s development was infamously rocky. With Hidetaka Miyazaki busy on Bloodborne, Dark Souls II was treated to a carousel of directors as the studio kept finding itself unsatisfied. Not only did this give us what’s often considered the runt of the series, but troves of cut content. Even if it didn’t make it to the final game, uncovering this abandoned material only takes a bit of digging. Before long, you’re deep underground.

“If you know very little about the subject of Souls modding, every Dark Souls game has something called Map Studio Binary,” says Doneda in a new video. “The map studio binary is what actually tells the game what to load and what not to load in that map… When they cut content from the game, especially when it’s about map, geometry, collision, and so on, they don’t actually delete these assets. They just remove them from the map studio binary. So, the engine of the game does not know that they should be loaded in there.”

Not just discovered, but restored, appears to be the original vision for the Gutter. Reminiscent of Blighttown, the Gutter was cavernous and ramshackle. Impaired vision and perilous drops making it one of the more contentious Dark Souls locations. It appears that at one point the Gutter was far more ambitious. Instead of mere twig scaffolding, there was once an entire civilization. Even devoid of enemies and items, the vast pillars of megastructures and ornate passages showcase what could have been.

In the nearly hour-long tour of this area, Doneda comes across notes in kanji, boss arenas and even a tavern. The allure of what’s available makes Doneda feel as if the developers hoped someone would one day come across this space.



https://kotaku.com/modder-discovers-abandoned-dark-souls-ii-level-even-more-sewers-2000688887
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Casey Affleck-Led Bitcoin Thriller Made By AI Seems Desperate For Some Sucker Studio To Buy It
item.content:encoded:
It’s clear that Hollywood studios want to use AI in movie making. How to do so remains elusive, as the tech’s shortcomings become immediately obvious in practice and prohibitively expensive even for devout firms. Still, even a half-baked dynamic could fetch a decent price tag. The Bourne Identity and Jumper director Doug Liman is taking his AI-generated film to this year’s Cannes, looking for a buyer. And as long as we’re being painfully on the nose, the movie is about bitcoin.

The Wrap’s Emily Zemler visited the set of Bitcoin: Killing Satoshi. The thriller follows model turned reporter (Gal Gadot) being recruited by a crypto millionaire (Pete Davidson) to investigate a doctor (Casey Affleck) who may be Satoshi Nakamoto, the alias of bitcoin’s elusive creator. Filmed out of a car showroom, the actors and outfits are real, but all the lighting, sets and post-production will be assigned to generative AI.

“We decided to use AI very early on,” Ryan Kavanaugh tells Zemler. “We budgeted out what it would be to do it practically and it was over $300 million. It has about 200 distinct locations, from Antarctica to Antigua to Vegas, which is obviously unproducible. We realized we could bring down the cost by utilizing some of the AI tools out there.”

Clearly seeking a middle ground with skeptics and labor groups, Bitcoin still employed 200 crew, half of which are cast members. Chroma key productions have been the norm for a while, the novelty here being that the task of replacing green screens with the final product will be handled by 55 “AI artists.” Producer Lawrence Grey tells The Wrap that they’re using AI “the right way.”

Even if it impresses the board, AI cheerleaders are constantly finding out that the public do not view the technology favorably, nor looking to pay ticket price for it. Disney recently laid off 1,000 staff at Marvel Studios, hitting their effects team hardest. It forecasts where studios would like production trends to bend.

The current play is marketing your use of AI to cut a deal with anyone willing to invest in it, regardless if the tech will every pay out. This happens to be what Casey Affleck’s brother Ben did with Netflix just last month for the sum of $600 million. All the funnier to pin this project on a thriller on bitcoin, another tax on the gullible, about the identity of Nakamoto, who The New York Times recently revealed may be British computer scientist Adam Back.



https://kotaku.com/bitcoin-thriller-billing-itself-as-first-studio-quality-ai-generated-movie-pete-davidson-gal-gadot-2000688877
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: No Rest for the Wicked Devs Haven’t Forgotten Consoles
item.content:encoded:
Moon Studios reminds players that the console versions of No Rest for the Wicked are still in development as development progresses toward version 1.0.

No Rest for the Wicked is an action role-playing game that is currently in Early Access on Steam. Despite being in Early Access for 2 years, the game has managed to captivate players worldwide. Recently, the team celebrated selling 1.5 million copies. Even since that announcement in February, that number has moved up, and the team is now celebrating its two-year anniversary on the market.

No Rest for the Wicked Devs Say They Haven’t Forgotten Console Players

In a post celebrating 2 years since the game entered Early Access, the developers of No Rest for the Wicked stated on Steam that they are still hard at work on the game’s full launch. They also took the time to assure console players that they haven’t been forgotten.

Furthermore, the developers at Moon Studios stated that they wouldn’t be slowing down and they’re proud of the game that they’ve built. They also thanked players not only for supporting them but also for challenging them to do better.

“It’s been TWO YEARS since No Rest for the Wicked entered Early Access. Since then, this game has grown and evolved in ways we could only pull off because of you.

“1.7 million copies sold, and counting. You’ve supported us, challenged us, and pushed us to do better. That’s exactly how we like it. We’re hard at work toward 1.0. And console players, we haven’t forgotten you,” a portion of the message reads.

Moon Studios, which was known prior to the release of this ARPG for the Ori series, plans to raise the game’s price to $60 when the full version finally launches. Learn more about the reasoning behind that decision at this link.

Sound off on this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/no-rest-for-the-wicked-devs-havent-forgotten-consoles/

item.title: DC Universe Online's Superman Day Event Is Live Through April 27
item.content:encoded:
DC Universe Online is marking Superman's first appearance in Action Comics with its annual Superman Day Event, running now through April 27. If you've been looking for a reason to log back in, Daybreak is handing you one with cosmetics drawn from two very different corners of the Superman mythos.

The event pulls its aesthetic from both Absolute Superman and Superman Beyond, and that split actually makes for an interesting reward lineup. On the unlimited side, you're looking at the Beyond Kryptonian Slimline Chest and Legs Styles alongside emblems from both the Absolute and Beyond lines. One-time account rewards include ten Flawless Nth Metal, 10,000 Ally Favor, 50 Stabilizer Fragments, and supply boxes for both Mogo Sprouts and Source Marks. Characters also get a one-time Skill Point Token, which is the kind of quiet, practical reward that tends to matter more than the cosmetics for players working through their builds.

Content-wise, the event funnels players into Doomed Metropolis for five open world daily missions, a set of weekly missions including bounties for both Heroes and Villains, and a slate of featured instances spanning solo, duo, alert, and raid formats. Last Son of Krypton, Earth 3: The Visitor, Phantom Zone, and the Doomsday raid are all on the table. This hits hot on the heels of the springtime event that released earlier this month, shoving a ton of content out in short order.

While DCUO has a few sales going on, you can pick up some freebies with code giveaways (such as the one above) throughout the week, so keep your eyes peeled on the game's socials. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/dc-universe-onlines-superman-day-event-is-live-through-april-27-2000137846
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Little Guy Solves Big Puzzles In Gecko Gods
item.content:encoded:
I usually have a low tolerance for “just a little guy” games. But the moment it’s about a bird or small reptile instead of a mouse or dog, I’m afraid you have me cornered. That’s just one of the reasons I’ve had my eye trained on Gecko Gods, a vibrantly colorful adventure about ancient temples, island obstacles, and a tiny teal lizard using a personal sailboat.

In Gecko Gods, you play as a small reptile washed ashore on a mysterious, serene island. Across this archipelago, you come across a vast network of abandoned temples. Within are statues and shrines seemingly built to deities that share your likeness, albeit 900 times larger than you. Overcoming your stature, you’ll push, pull, and prod your way around ancient mechanisms to unlock new chambers and strange magics. Gobble insects, battle sentries, and board your own boat to turn a big world larger, especially for a squat creature such as yourself.

Gorgeous pastel tones, breezy sounds and relaxed landscapes betray a game that can still lash its tongue. Even medium-sized threats can prove to be a difficult challenge at your height. The ability to make bold leaps and climb across ceilings invites you to think a little more three dimensionally about dungeon puzzles and exploration, at least compared to your usual Zelda fare.

For those who enjoy tomb raiding on their own terms, or open world adventure games pared down on quests and collectables, Gecko Gods is a charming afternoon expedition. Plus, us lizard-heads finally getting some more options out here. Watch in real time as my cute animal video gaming reservations melt away. And if you’d happen to prefer a gecko-starring game with wisenheimer jokes about Bruce Vilanch, well as it happens there are a surprising amount of video games for you too.

Gecko Gods is now available on Steam, PlayStation and Nintendo Switch.



https://kotaku.com/little-guy-solves-big-puzzles-in-gecko-gods-steam-indie-game-2000688872
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Windrose Sells 1 Million Copies And Hits New Player Milestone
item.content:encoded:
Windrose continues to find success, currently ranking fourth among Steam’s most-played games. The Kraken Express title was released on April 14. Since that time, they have amassed over 7,000 reviews with a very positive overall score.

The game is a pirate-themed survival, crafting, open-world PvE game, and it’s so strongly connected with players that it currently has 220,718 players at the time of this writing. The only three games with more concurrent players are Counter-Strike 2, Dota 2, and PUBG: Battlegrounds.

Kraken Express Expresses Gratitude for Windrose’s Success

In a Twitter post, the development team not only commented on the concurrent player milestone but also announced that the game had sold over 1 million copies. Additionally, they expressed their gratitude to the player base for not only supporting the game but also for their patience as they continue to work on fixes for the title.

“Ahoy, captains! We have just plundered another milestone – 1,000,000 copies, and “we” means “you” – a crew of fine brave captains sailing the seas of Windrose. Moreover, today Windrose has assembled over 200,000 concurrent players! Your support for the game is absolutely astonishing, and your patience while we are working on fixes is truly heart-warming. While we keep working on the game, we read your reviews and comments, watch your videos and streams, and sometimes, honestly, shed a pirate-y tear of happiness, because now when the game is out you share our dream of swashbuckling adventure. And there are boars, too, we know, but whatever does not send you flying into the nearest tree, makes you stronger, right?

“Thank you for this, captains. You are our inspiration in this journey, and YARRRRR, that’s going to be an exciting one,” the team stated in a separate message on Steam.

Windrose was originally announced at the PC Gaming Show: Most Wanted Showcase in December 2025 and had great success during a Steam Next Fest demo in February. Now, they just have to keep the sales afloat.

What do you think about the game so far if you played it? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/windrose-sells-1-million-copies-and-hits-new-player-milestone/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Windrose Celebrates 1 Million Copies Sold as Player Counts Climb
item.content:encoded:
Cooperative pirate survival game Windrose has reached 1 million copies sold less than a week after its Early Access launch for PC.

Pocketpair Publishing and Kraken Express announced the sales milestone with an update post on Steam today. It’s an announcement that saw it sail past the marker six days after launching for PC via the Valve platform and Epic Games Store April 14.

Reaching 1 million copies is a feat worth celebrating for Kraken as it charts a course through Early Access development, especially as its concurrent player count on Steam alone continues to climb. It’s managed to climb to 200,000 active pirates on Steam alone, with SteambDB recording a peak of 211,177 at the time of this piece’s publication.

Thank you! The boar sends its two-tusked regards.

Jokes aside, your support has been incredible, and your patience while we work on fixes means the world to us.

Moreover, Windrose surpassed 200,000 concurrent players on Steam!

Thank you kindly, on behalf of our entire studio. pic.twitter.com/axHyxc8wQU

— Windrose (@PlayWindrose) April 19, 2026

"Your support for the game is absolutely astonishing, and your patience while we are working on fixes is truly heart-warming," Kraken said. "While we keep working on the game, we read your reviews and comments, watch your videos and streams, and sometimes, honestly, shed a pirate-y tear of happiness, because now when the game is out you share our dream of swashbuckling adventure."

Windrose, previously known as Crosswind, debuted to ‘Very Positive’ reviews when it embarked on its Early Access journey last week. Kraken sells it as a "PvE survival adventure in the Age of Piracy," telling fans it will work with them through the development process in the hopes of reaching a 1.0 launch sometime in the next "1.5 to 2.5 years."

Players can head out on the sea alone or play with friends via co-op, with the developers estimating the final release will feature "around 50% more content." We said Windrose "made a strong first impression” and praised its “exploration and progression" in our review so far last week.

While Windrose charges ahead, you can see our 10 essential ship tips. You can also see our best suggestions for how to quickly farm bells.

Michael Cripe is a freelance writer with IGN. He's best known for his work at sites like The Pitch, The Escapist, and OnlySP. Be sure to give him a follow on Bluesky (@mikecripe.bsky.social) and Twitter (@MikeCripe).

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/windrose-celebrates-1-million-copies-sold-as-player-counts-climb
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Celebrate MapleStory's 21st Anniversary With The Maple University Event
item.content:encoded:
The classic MMORPG MapleStory is celebrating its 21st anniversary with Maple University, a scholarly-themed event that has players taking on monsters and mini-games. 

Maple University offers two major tracks to follow. The Basic Lecture lets you complete Gen Ed, Majors and Internships to get Credits for Rewards. Meanwhile, the Special Lectures let players spend Mu Gong and Ephenia Points accumulated from the Basic Lecture for progression buffs. 

It’s not without perks, though, as the Rowdy Campus Life and Glorious Graduation skills are activated by a full gauge of killed monsters to give an EXP boost. Some buffs are also offered by the Hasty Hunting MAX event with its own missions to tackle, and further buffs are offered by Mano by exploring the university and picking up the Scholarship Kit.

A little Club Fair takes place during this event, with six mini-games offered to let players earn EXP Coupons and furniture. Players will be able to vote in the Monster Election, essentially a team progression event where players will complete activities including Club Fair mini-games to progress their candidates’ progress and gain unique perks per candidate. There’s also a customizable student ID for the event. 

Full details on everything available for MapleStory’s 21st anniversary event are available on the official web page for the event. 

In the meantime, Nexon has been running the Closed Beta Test for the Classic World edition of the game. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/celebrate-maplestorys-21st-anniversary-with-the-maple-university-event-2000137844
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Gustave Actor Wants Robert Pattinson in Expedition 33 Film
item.content:encoded:
The actor who played Gustave in Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has named the actor he would like to see portray the character on the big screen. However, there’s a catch. He still wants to provide the voice.

In 2025, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 captured the hearts and minds of players. The Sandfall Interactive title set a new standard for AA video games and what “independent” games can produce. A large part of that was due to the portrayal of the characters by the talented performers who gave voice to the story.

Charlie Cox Would Like to See Robert Pattinson in a Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Movie

Charlie Cox received critical acclaim for his portrayal of Gustave. Part of that acclaim included the game winning another Game of the Year award at the 2026 BAFTA Games Awards. Ahead of the event, Cox was asked by GamesRadar+ if he’d like to reprise his role one day.

Cox says he would absolutely be down for such a moment. Still, he believes that if the game were to be made into a movie, the part should be performed by Robert Pattinson, with the catch that he would still provide the voice.

“My involvement with this, as minuscule as it was originally and how it’s now expanded so much, has been nothing but an incredibly humbling and enjoyable experience,” he tells us. “So, yeah, absolutely, yeah. I was thinking that they should probably – if they make the movie – they should get Robert Pattinson to play the part, and I should do the voice. Because then they would have pretty much the exact Gustave from the game.”

There you have it. The “Twilight” and “The Batman” star now has the endorsement of Cox to play Gustave on the big screen.

To check out the full list of winners from the 2026 BAFTA Games Awards, click here.

Would you like to see a movie about Expedition 33? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/gustave-actor-wants-robert-pattinson-in-expedition-33-film/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Indie MMO Spotlight: Closing The Book Of Travels
item.content:encoded:
Mitch Gassner Updated: Apr 19, 2026 1:47 PM Posted: Apr 19, 2026 11:00 AM ET
Category: Columns Indie MMO Spotlight 0

Each week, we scour the internet for all things indie MMO-related, so you don’t have to. This week, it’s bad news for Book of Travels, Erenshor celebrates its first anniversary, and Scars of Honor reworks skill trees. Plenty of other stuff is also going on, so let’s quit wasting time and get started with this week’s Indie MMO Spotlight.

Book of Travels

It has been quite a while since we heard anything about Book of Travels, so it probably isn’t a surprise that when Might and Delight surfaced this week, it was bad news for the Tiny MMO. The servers will be shutting down in July, but there is a sliver of good news in that BoT will be playable offline.

Broken Ranks

We have news of another update delay, this time from White Moon Games. The team has delayed the next end-game content update. The reason given is very simple - they don’t want to release content that isn’t ready. At this time, there still isn’t an ETA for when the team expects to finally release the content.

Corepunk

The Player Market Orders release has been moved to next week for further testing and polish. Along with work on internal systems, the team didn’t take into account just how many core systems the PMO interacts with, which required more testing than usual.

Eldramoor: Haven in the Mist

Eldramoor’s Alpha ends on April 20th. If you want more information about Eldramoor as you wait for Beta later this year, you may be interested in the new Acolyte of Knowledge membership the team is now offering. For $4.99 per month, you get an exclusive Discord role, dev sneak peeks, a 5% merch discount, and exclusive merch items. 

Erenshor

It’s hard to believe that Erenshor, the single-player simulated MMORPG is already celebrating its first anniversary. A recent blog post takes a look back at the previous year before taking a look at what’s in store for the next year.

Eterspire

In its most recent bi-weekly patch, we get new quests and Silver and Gold Slayer Cloak rewards for beating Remnant bosses 250 and 500 times.

Farwoods

Don’t forget to keep your hygiene up in-game or else you might contract one of the new diseases that has been added to Farwoods. The common cold, influenza, and pneumonia are all implemented to test out the new disease mechanics. New herbs have also been added to treat any diseases you catch.

Hytale

Update 5 Patch 4 is now live. Along with many other items, the patch adds a Friends List and Rich Presence.

Loftia

The Official Beta Cinematic Trailer was released on Friday. Closed Beta officially starts on Wednesday, April 22nd. 

Once Human

New changes are coming to RaidZone. The biggest change is there will now be three modes: casual, standard, and hardcore.

Project Gorgon

A new update was released on April 14th. The update includes Prodigy adjustments, minor changes to Vampirism, Statehelm changes, and much more.

Prosperous Universe

Michi discusses the new post-tutorial contract series in Development Log #526.

Realm of the Mad God

Season 28 Part 1: Hydroflow is live. Season 28 will run for 7 weeks.

Scars of Honor

The Paladin Talen Tree has received a major overhaul. The new tree will be available during the April 30th playtest.

Swordeye

Along with several small updates this week, a new video showcases some incoming combat tweaks.

Villagers & Heroes

Patch 5.66: Equinox is now live. The patch notes show a bunch of new rewards as well as some other changes to the zone.

Wild Terra 2: New Lands

Major Update 2.6.425 is live. The update adds new house building parts and the armor fortification mechanic.


Mitch Gassner

Part-time game reviewer, full-time gaming geek, Mitch was introduced to Pac-Man and Asteroids at Shakey's Pizza in the '70s and has been hooked on games ever since. Mitch has always had an opinion to share on anything gaming, but it wasn't until 2018 that he began his writing side hustle. Mitch currently writes about gaming and tech for MMORPG and Gamespace.com. You can find him on X @mitchgassner.



https://www.mmorpg.com/columns/indie-mmo-spotlight-closing-the-book-of-travels-2000137845
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Strands #777 Hints and Answers for Today, April 19, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #777 for Saturday, April 19, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 19, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #777 are all related to terms you use to describe small changes.

General Strands Hints For April 19

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: A word that means to do better.
  • Hint 2: An anagram of “later.”
  • Hint 3: Steam supports a community that does this to games.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #777 for April 19, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 18, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • DOME
  • DOMAIN
  • MAIN
  • FIXED
  • THER

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #777 On April 19, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • ALTER
  • MODIFY
  • IMPROVE
  • REFINE
  • TWEAK
  • ADJUST
  • SPANAGRAM: THEREIFIXEDIT

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #777?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 20 will be Strands Game #778.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-777-hints-and-answers-for-today-april-19-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem dataminer finds best clue yet that a Mercenaries mode could be on the way
item.content:encoded:
A dataminer believes they've discovered unused music hidden in the backend of Resident Evil Requiem, suggesting it "could be related to [a] future extra mode or Mercenaries".

Syrkov shared their findings on X/Twitter over the weekend, including instructions on how you too can find the mysterious music if you're prepared to explore the game files.

"I was digging through Resident Evil Requiem files and found something that could be related to future extra mode / Mercenaries. Here's some of the tracks I found," they wrote, appending their findings (thanks, IGN).

Mercenaries, for the uninitiated, is the recurring blood-pumping arcade shooting minigame that accompanied the release of Resident Evil 3, Resident Evil 4, and Resident Evil 5. It usually involves you fighting to stay alive as you fight off hordes of zombies/Ganados, trying to amass as big a score as possible to show off on a global leaderboard.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

While the first two tracks are not particularly out of place (the second sounds like the safe room music?), and third is 'just' a clicking clock/countdown(?!), the fourth sounds very much like it could come from a frantic Mercenaries stage, or even a boss fight. Interestingly, it continues the clicking sound from Track 3, suggesting you're up against a time limit.

Tracks 7 and 9 similarly sound like they would be right at home in a Mercenaries level.

Could the "mini-game" we're expected next month be a Mercenaries mode? Or does this music relate to the new story expansion that director Koshi Nakanishi said would "delve deeper into the world of Requiem" when it arrives? Hopefully we don't have too much longer to wait.

Last month, the Resident Evil series celebrated its 30th anniversary. At this time, Resident Evil series executive producer Jun Takeuchi promised us all that "even more wonderful experiences" are coming.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/resident-evil-requiem-dataminer-finds-best-clue-yet-that-a-mercenaries-mode-could-be-on-the-way
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: New Capture-The-Flag Game Last Flag, Led By Imagine Dragons' Gamer Singer, Is Running A Demo This Weekend
item.content:encoded:
Led by the lead vocalist of the hit band Imagine Dragons, the multiplayer 5-versus-5 capture-the-flag title Last Flag is showing off its chops just after release by launching a free demo weekend through Steam. 

Going on now, players can download the demo of the game and give it a go until Monday, April 20 at 10:00 a.m. PDT. The title came out this past Wednesday, and while we take Steam reviews with a grain of salt, it’s sitting at Mostly Positive reviews, decent for a celebrity-backed studio debut title.  

There’s a decent sale on it that brings it down to $11.99, which is generous given it’s already a decent $14.99 and it’s only just launched. The only DLC so far is a $4.99 cosmetic pack, which is also on sale for $3.99 until Monday, and a soundtrack priced the same.  

It appears the team is also gearing up to release a new map called Twin Temples, themed to ruins in the Amazonian jungles, debuting “early this Summer.” 

MMORPG was able to join a hands-on demo last year, where our Managing Editor enjoyed not only the vibes of classic CTF video games, but also the sharp focus on objectives mixed with exciting shooter gameplay. 

Plus, we chatted with Imagine Dragons’s manager about how developing a game at all was more of a bucket-list item than a desire to get some money, as the manager knew some 3D modeling and the lead singer knows some coding. After all, the band once touted their enjoyment of League of Legends to the point where they wrote two now-legendary tracks for the MOBA-franchise, including the 2014 songs “Warriors” that started the esports division’s annual “anthem” tradition. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/new-capture-the-flag-game-last-flag-led-by-imagine-dragons-gamer-singer-is-running-a-demo-this-weekend-2000137842
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite UEFN Gets AI-Powered NPC Conversations: Epic Taps Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite and ElevenLabs
item.content:encoded:
Remember Fortnite's AI-powered Darth Vader that led SAG-AFTRA to file an unfair labor practice charge against Epic Games? Well, the studio has now revealed the latest evolution of the technology.

Originally described as a "Persona device", the tech is now simply called "conversations", but the core remains the same: Fortnite developers can use conversations to create AI-powered characters with personalities and voices that can talk, react, respond to player actions, and even influence gameplay.

Released with Fortnite's v40.20 update (launched on April 16), it is available in the Unreal Editor for Fortnite (UEFN) and allows island creators to build AI-powered NPCs capable of unscripted, real-time dialogue with players. Creators define a character's personality, knowledge base, and behavior using prompts with as few as 20 lines of text. The underlying system is, of course, LLM-powered, generating responses dynamically based on what players say and do. Creators can also customize voice type, tone, delivery style, and personality traits.

The system goes far beyond simple conversations, according to Epic, as NPCs can react to and remember player actions within a session. They can drive gameplay outcomes; for example, a guard NPC could be persuaded to grant access to a restricted area. They may even trigger in-game events, dynamically adjust difficulty based on performance, or serve as adaptive narrators or commentators.

Within UEFN, Epic leverages Scene Graph and the Verse API. However, as mentioned earlier in the article, the tech is also largely powered by LLMs. Indeed, Epic confirmed that they are using Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite model to process audio inputs and generate text responses, and ElevenLabs to convert these into voiced outputs. These are some of the best models on the market for those respective use cases.

Epic clarified that it will not store any player audio. Moreover, the addition of conversations has required Epic to update its Fortnite Developer Rule set with the following clauses:

  • Do not create a persona designed to provide medical or mental health guidance.
  • Do not create a persona that role-plays as, simulates, or impersonates a date, romantic partner, or other intimate companion.
  • Do not attempt to circumvent or undermine Epic's safety systems, including intentionally designing your persona to bypass Epic's content restrictions.

Developers who break these rules will receive enforcement actions, up to and including an account ban.

Notably, conversations is currently considered an Experimental feature. As such, Fortnite developers won't be able to publish islands featuring AI NPCs at this time, at least until the feature reaches Beta state. The voice models are also not final, and the LLMs' response times are slower than expected for the final release. During this Experimental phase, Epic plans to improve voice quality, reduce latency, and adjust the default NPC behavior.

On the flip side, it seems that the testing use of both Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite and ElevenLabs, which is not free, is currently shouldered by Epic. Whether this continues once the feature goes live to the public remains to be seen.

If you're a Fortnite developer and wish to learn more about these AI-powered NPCs, Epic has released four pages of documentation:

  • LLM conversations
  • Developing Personas
  • Designing a Conversation
  • Using the Prompt Editor Tool

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/fortnite-uefn-ai-npc-conversations-gemini-elevenlabs/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Surprise! Nintendo launches new pop-up store in London
item.content:encoded:
Mario's guest-starring at Argos on Tottenham Court Road for a limited-time only.

Surprise! Nintendo has launched a pop-up store in London.

The Nintendo Experience Zone, available now in the Argos Store on Tottenham Court Road, offers a range of goodies, from games and consoles to plushies, figures, and collectibles from right across Nintendo's impressive IP catalogue.

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

According to BadxGurlCosplay, who shared the news via TikTok (thanks, VGC), the store is "fully stocked with the latest games and consoles as well as all of the accessories to go with it".

"You'll also find some merch that is exclusive to Argos. There's also IPs like Zelda, Metroid, Animal Crossing, Kirby, Splatoon, and Pikmin," they added. "There's also a huge range of Pokémon peripherals, accessories, plushies, as well as toys and board games. There is also a really nice range of stationery, homeware, and kids' toys."

"After seven months of planning, our Argos Nintendo Tottenham Court Road partnership is live, bringing together hundreds of lines from across our gaming and license partners," said Argos’ gaming buyer, Peter Wray, on LinkedIn.

"What an amazing project to have landed on. Big thanks to our amazing partners – some of whom even hand-delivered stock to us!"

It's unclear how long the store will be operating, but VGC claims it'll be running for "the next few months".

Last month, Nintendo announced sweeping changes to its first-party published games moving forward: digital and physical copies will now sell at different prices. After a lot of confusion and not an insignificant outrage, Nintendo clarified: "The cost of physical games is not going up.

"This means that when Nintendo sells digital versions of Nintendo-published games exclusive to Nintendo Switch 2 to consumers in the US, those prices will have an MSRP that is lower than their physical counterparts. Retail partners set their own prices for physical and digital games, and pricing for each title may vary."

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/surprise-nintendo-launches-new-pop-up-store-in-london
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: This Final Fantasy Card Is A Devious Addition To A Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon
item.content:encoded:
This Final Fantasy Card Is A Devious Addition To A Secrets of Strixhaven Commander Precon

Have some goad with your goad.

Magic: The Gathering’s Secrets of Strixhaven set is fast approaching (with prerelease this weekend), and for the first time since Tarkir: Dragonstorm, we’re getting five Commander precons with it.

One of the more intriguing options of the bunch is Silverquill Influence, a white/black deck that blends artifacts and auras with a Goad theme – and there’s one Final Fantasy card that slots in beautifully.

This Final Fantasy Card Is Spiking In Price

Eye of Nidhogg

Eye of Nidhogg is a Legendary Enchantment — Aura that costs one Black mana and two generic.

It reads “Enchant creature. Enchanted creature is a black Dragon with base power and toughness 4/2, has flying and deathtouch, and is goaded.”

If you’ve not come up against a Goad-focused deck before, the idea is that it pushes players to attack each other instead of the one goading them.

This enchantment also triggers Killian, Decisive Mentor, the Silverquill Influence deck’s Commander, because as Eye of Nidhogg enters, Killian can goad something else. So for three mana, you’re goading twice, and triggering Killian’s card draw.

Better still, once the creature Eye of Nidhogg is on dies, the card bounces back to your hand ready to create even more chaos.

The card was selling for a pittance not long ago, but its value has jumped 3,550% according to TCGplayer, making it arund $7.50 at the time of writing.

As a reminder, each Commander precon from the set is packed with reprint value and still going for $49.99 each at Amazon (the MSRP).

For more on Magic: The Gathering, be sure to check out the new Commanders joining Killian in the set, and expect plenty more coverage of Secrets of Strixhaven as the set gets closer.

Lloyd Coombes is an experienced freelancer in tech, gaming and fitness seen at Polygon, Eurogamer, Macworld, TechRadar and many more. He's a big fan of Magic: The Gathering and other collectible card games, much to his wife's dismay.

Recommends

In This Article



https://www.ign.com/articles/this-final-fantasy-card-is-a-devious-addition-to-a-secrets-of-strixhaven-commander-precon
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ghost of Yōtei's Hell Mode boss battle difficulty is now live
item.content:encoded:
Patch 1.601 introduces raid updates, gear retirement, stability fixes, and gameplay tweaks.

Ghost of Yōtei's Hell Mode boss battle difficulty is now here, along with a load of QoL and performance fixes, plus Raid matchmaking.

Patch 1.601, which rolled out over the weekend, shows developer Sucker Punch is still committed to stabilising and polishing its action-adventure game six months on from release. It introduces a number of interesting tweaks, including the ability to matchmake Raid missions and skip straight to the Dragon and Saito raid boss fights.

Here's everything you can expect in Patch 1.601:

Raid Updates

  • “Hell Mode” boss battle difficulty for the Raid is now live.
  • Add new class cosmetics for defeating Hell Mode bosses.
  • Enable matchmaking for Raid missions.
  • Add “Skip straight to the Raid Boss” options for Dragon and Saito.

Gear Retirement

  • Add “Gear Retirement” prestige feature.
  • Players can spend in-game currency to retire perfected gear items. There is no gameplay advantage, but gear retirement count is visible to other players, and attracts fireflies when bowing.

Performance and Stability

  • Various rare crash fixes.

Gameplay

  • Fix issue where certain enemies could occasionally do extra damage on some attacks.
  • Added "Matchmake to fill empty slots" in weekly Nightmare missions.
  • Prevent players from being disarmed during Ultimate attacks.
  • Reduced Tanegashima damage vs bosses.
  • Reduced Lightning Tanegashima headshot damage.
  • Fix issue where Samurai and Shinobi could have their Ultimates canceled when being attacked.
  • Enable petting the spirit animals in Survival.
  • Enable emotes while waiting at gathering points
  • Fix issue where Bo-Hiya Rain card could cause players to die after completing an Incursion mission.
  • Don't allow Giant Bo-Hiya to drop inside defend areas in Story missions.
  • Uncompleted daily bounties now refresh daily.
  • Prevent Shadow Snake from Attuning himself.
  • Added a checkmark on completed Story and Incursion missions in the Campaign menu.
  • Enable weapon trails in Lobby Bamboo Strike.
  • Fix leaderboards UI issue where the list would not scroll past the first page.

Ghost of Yōtei is Sucker Punch's follow-up to Ghost of Tsushima, set in northern Japan around 300 years after the first title. We awarded it 3/5 stars, writing in Eurogamer's Ghost of Yōtei review: "Sucker Punch's sequel offers more great swordplay and heartfelt storytelling, but would be better served as a linear action game, freed of its poor sidequests and dated open world."

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/ghost-of-yoteis-hell-mode-boss-battle-difficulty-is-now-live
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: YouTuber Reconstructs Nintendo’s Notoriously Rare First Arcade Game
item.content:encoded:
It’s common knowledge that Nintendo made novelty toys and Hanafuda cards long before they arrived to video games. While they reshaped the industry, their games prior to Donkey Kong are less renowned. There’s good reason for that. Wild Gunman, their first arcade game, is notoriously rare. There is no evidence that a working cabinet exists anywhere in the world. Until now. A Canadian arcade repairman and YouTuber took on the challenge of reconstructing one after coming into possession of its most integral parts.

“One day in July 2025, as I was browsing the arcade parts category on eBay i came across an auction that didn’t make a lot of sense to me,” said Callan “74XX” Brown in his video, “a set of Nintendo branded film reels.”

Dating back to 1974, Wild Gunman was Nintendo’s first arcade game, but it was not a video game per se. Using two simultaneous film reel projectors, the light gun western would alternate between them depending on where you shot, triggering a winning or losing state. Making the game, Gunpei Yokoi requested Fuji use Tetoron, a polyester mix that could be more durable. That said, Yokoi still believed it would begin to wear out after 1,000 play sessions, never mind attract modes. While the full motion game was a novel hit at launch, its lifetime ability to pull a profit seemed dubious, nevermind the video invasion and advent Laserdiscs.

Surviving copies of the Wild Gunman reels are incredibly rare. In 2021, historian Kate Willært believed that the cleanest footage of Nintendo’s first game wasn’t in arcades, but in an underground experimental short film and the boneheaded ‘80s comedy Gas. Later that year, a collector came across two of the four film reels.

Puzzled by seeing film reels in an arcade auction, Brown noticed the quality Nintendo control stickers, painting them as the legitimate article. It’s possible these were the holy grail. After placing a winning bid and buying a classroom 16mm projector from a nearby town (thank you Ontario education system), Brown was able to watch the Wild Gunman reels, likely their first screening in 40 years.

Getting the footage digitized, Brown then got to work reconstructing Nintendo’s historic cabinet himself. Reverse engineering patents and making use of open source software, he’s built a magnificent modernized replica of Wild Gunman. With video projections from scans of the original stock, the elusive films will be spared from further distress. Brown says he intends to showcase his work in local conventions, such as Ontario PinFest.

“Thank you for joining me on my journey to make what might be the only playable Wild Gunman ‘74 in North America,” says Brown, “maybe the world.”



https://kotaku.com/youtuber-reconstructs-nintendos-notoriously-rare-first-arcade-game-2000688868
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: HBO's The Last of Us TV show casts Yara and Lev's mother, Miriam
item.content:encoded:
Sinners star Li Jun Li joins Season 3 as a member of the Seraphites.

Sinners star Li Jun Li joins The Last of Us Season 3 as Miriam, Yara and Lev's mother.

We already knew that Michelle Mao would portray Yara and Kyriana Kratter as Lev, but Variety has now revealed that Li Jun Li will play their Seraphite parent.

Li joins Jason Ritter (WLF soldier), Patrick Wilson (Jerry), Ariela Barer (Mel), Tati Gabrielle (Nora), and Spencer Lord (Owen) in the critically-acclaimed series' third season, alongside Clea DuVall (Seraphite) and Jorge Lendeborg Jr. (Manny), as well as Bella Ramsey (Ellie), Kaitlyn Dever (Abby), Isabella Merced (Dina), Gabriel Luna (Tommy), and Jeffery Wright (Isaac).

Li has previously portrayed Grace Chow in Sinners and Iris Chang in Quantico.

The Last of Us season three will move its focus from Bella Ramsey's Ellie, and instead tell Abby's side of the story in Seattle. It will also introduce a number of new characters for the show, such as Abby's father Jerry (played by Patrick Wilson), Michelle Mao's Yara and Kyriana Kratter's Lev. Filming began on season three at the end of last month. We got sneak peeks at Kaitlyn Dever's Abby and Kyriana Kratter's Lev on set.

Naughty Dog's Neil Druckmann announced last year that he would be stepping back from the series after The Last of Us season two to focus on games, including Intergalactic: The Heretic Prophet. Druckmann had previously worked on The Last of Us' adaptation as a co-showrunner, director, writer and producer.

We don't yet have a release date for The Last of Us' third season beyond 2027. However, the upcoming season is also suspected to be the show's last. HBO's CEO Casey Bloy has said he is not worried about The Last of Us without Druckmann, stating the game director had "given us a good blueprint with the show".

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/hbos-the-last-of-us-tv-show-casts-yara-and-levs-mother-miriam
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Crimson Desert dev thanks you for your patience as it takes a little extra time to "test and polish" its next big patch
item.content:encoded:
Crimson Desert developer Pearl Abyss has warned it will be "tak[ing its] time as it preps the "larger" upcoming update, thanking players in advance for their patience.

Up until now, Pearl Abyss has been working double-time to crank out updates pretty much every week, with around nine full and hotfix updates issued since the RPG launched on 19th March.

However, while you'd have been forgiven for expecting the patch Pearl Abyss detailed last week to roll out this weekend as usual, the team did pop up on social media yesterday to let players know there'll be a delay while it "takes its time" testing and polishing.

Hey Greymanes,
We’re currently preparing our next update which will include several features mentioned in last week's Dev Update notice, including the addition of a keyboard/mouse and controller preset, difficulty settings, category tabs for your inventory, and more. We want to…

— Crimson Desert (@CrimsonDesert_) April 18, 2026

To see this content please enable targeting cookies.

"We're currently preparing our next update, which will include several features mentioned in last week's Dev Update notice, including the addition of a keyboard/mouse and controller preset, difficulty settings, category tabs for your inventory, and more," the brief statement began.

"We want to take our time with testing and polishing things before we roll the patch out, which we expect to do sometime next week. Also, due to the distant scenery quality improvements we are adding in, this patch size will be larger than our previous ones. We hope you're looking forward to this update, and thanks for your patience and support!"

As you'll see from the statement, there's been no firm date on when the new features will roll out, but we'll keep you posted.

This new update will introduce some huge features, such as the ability to replay boss fights, new difficulty levels, respawning enemy outposts, and new outfits.

Crimson Desert had a divisive but financially successful launch. In Eurogamer's Crimson Desert review, we wrote that the game constantly oscillates "between whoop-inducing bombast and utter brain-smoothing banality". Though substantial official patches have been arriving in quick succession, modders are also hard at work, even giving PC players the option to create their own female characters.

Related topics


https://www.eurogamer.net/crimson-desert-dev-thanks-you-for-your-patience-as-it-takes-a-little-extra-time-to-test-and-polish-its-next-big-patch
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Metal Slug 30th anniversary website launched, teases ‘Mission Reboot’
item.content:encoded:
30 years and still running.

SNK has launched a special website and released a retrospective video celebrating the 30th anniversary of side-scrolling run-and-gun action game series Metal Slug, which tease “Mission Reboot.”

“To celebrate 30 years of Metal Slug action, we’re reigniting and rebooting the series with a wide range of exciting projects—including new ventures in gaming!,” reads the official website.

Here is an overview of the series, via the special website:

30 years and still running.

Metal Slug‘s detailed pixel art and charming, lighthearted visuals set it apart from the rest when it hit arcades in 1996.

Players have been spellbound by the game’s vibrant graphics ever since—whether it be comically expressive soldiers and their nuanced movements, or giant war machines whose destruction shakes the entire screen. Coupled with its simple, intuitive controls and fast-paced gameplay, it’s little wonder this SNK classic garnered acclaim not just in the arcade but on home consoles as well.

Even now, 30 years on, the Metal Slug series continues to evolve. Still running, still gunning—with no end in sight.

Strap in and stay tuned for what lies on the horizon!

Watch the retrospective below.

Metal Slug 30th Anniversary Retrospective



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/metal-slug-30th-anniversary-website-launched-teases-mission-reboot
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
